name: CCC Intelligent Solutions Holdings Inc.

profile.description: CCC Intelligent Solutions is the leading cloud platform for the multi-trillion-dollar P&C insurance economy. The CCC Intelligent Experience Cloud connects more than 35,000 businesses including insurers, collision repair facilities, automakers, and parts suppliers, with AI representing approximately 10% of revenue.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): Full-year 2025 revenue of $1.057B (+12% YoY) with adjusted EBITDA of $436M (41% margin); Q4 revenue of $278M (+13%) exceeded guidance [1]. Gross dollar retention of 99% and net dollar retention of 106% demonstrate exceptional customer stickiness. AI Deployment (2/2): AI represents ~$100M in annual revenue (10% of total) across 125+ insurers and 15,000+ repair facilities, making CCC one of the few SaaS companies with proven AI revenue at scale [1]. Market Share (1/2): Dominant position in P&C insurance claims technology, connecting 35,000+ businesses; expanded TAM through EvolutionIQ acquisition entering disability and workers' comp [1]. Pipeline (1/2): 2026 guidance of $1.147-1.157B (+9% growth) with EBITDA margins expanding toward 80% long-term gross margin target; AI utilization still in low single digits, providing enormous growth runway [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): Appointed Josh Valdez as CPO in January 2026 specifically to scale AI-driven innovation; board addition of John Schweitzer for data platform expertise [2].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): CCC's mission to 'keep people's lives moving forward when it matters most' — helping accident victims get back on the road through efficient claims processing — is tangible and meaningful [2]. Community Impact (1/2): Connects 35,000+ businesses in the insurance ecosystem; the platform's efficiency reduces claim processing time and cost for millions of consumers annually. Employee Retention (1/2): CEO Githesh Ramamurthy has led since 1999 (26+ years), one of the longest-tenured CEOs in enterprise software; Senior Manager of Leadership & Learning Innovation role shows investment in people development [2]. Insider Buying (1/2): $500M share repurchase authorization including $300M accelerated repurchase demonstrates capital allocation conviction [1].

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: CCC's financials show a strong SaaS business profile with some valuation pressure. D/E of 78.0% is reasonable. Profit margin of 0.04% is essentially break-even on a GAAP basis due to SBC, though non-GAAP profitability is strong (41% EBITDA margin) [1]. ROE of 0.09% reflects GAAP accounting distortions. Forward P/E of 12.9x is attractive for a SaaS company with 12% growth and expanding margins. Revenue growth of 12.7% YoY with 85% subscription revenue creates predictable, durable cash flows [1]. SBC reduction from 17% to 13% of revenue in 2026 with single-digit trajectory by 2027. Liquidity floor cleared: 12.7M avg vol x $6.18 = $78.5M [1].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): AI is CCC's primary growth driver, not a threat. The company is one of the few enterprise software companies with ~$100M in proven AI revenue, deployed across 125+ insurers and 15,000+ repair facilities [1]. CCC's AI automates claims estimation, photo-based damage assessment, and fraud detection — tasks that require deep domain expertise and proprietary industry data that is extremely difficult for new entrants to replicate. 99% gross dollar retention demonstrates that customers cannot easily switch. Structural industry tailwinds (labor shortages, medical inflation, increasing claim complexity) drive demand for AI automation. The EvolutionIQ acquisition extends AI capabilities into disability and workers' comp. AI Opportunity (1/1): AI utilization remains in low single digits to low double digits — the growth runway from expanding usage within existing customers is enormous [1].

infinite_game.summary: CCC under CEO Ramamurthy's 26-year tenure exemplifies infinite game thinking in enterprise software. The company has patiently built a network-effects platform connecting 35,000+ businesses, achieving 99% retention while investing in AI as the next decade of growth. The focus on expanding the ecosystem rather than extracting maximum near-term revenue, and the EvolutionIQ acquisition to extend into adjacent verticals, show long-term strategic vision.

just_cause.analysis: CCC's mission to 'keep people's lives moving forward when it matters most' grounds the company in a tangible human need — getting accident victims back on the road efficiently (2 pts). The platform's role in reducing insurance friction for millions of consumers annually provides enduring purpose [2] (1 pt).

trusting_teams.analysis: CEO Ramamurthy's 26-year tenure is extraordinary for enterprise software, suggesting deep organizational trust (2 pts). The Senior Manager of Leadership & Learning Innovation role and investment in AI-enabled learning show commitment to workforce development [2].

worthy_rivals.analysis: CCC competes against Verisk, Mitchell, and emerging AI-native insurtechs but shows limited public acknowledgment of how these rivals sharpen its strategy (0 pts).

existential_flexibility.analysis: Evolved from auto claims software to a comprehensive P&C insurance platform, then expanded into disability/workers' comp via EvolutionIQ (1 pt). The pivot from perpetual licensing to cloud SaaS to AI-native platform shows continuous reinvention (1 pt) [1].

courage_to_lead.analysis: Investing heavily in AI (now 10% of revenue) while maintaining 41% EBITDA margins requires balancing growth and profitability with discipline (1 pt). The EvolutionIQ acquisition and $500M buyback show confidence in the platform's extensibility (1 pt) [1].

investment_thesis: CCC Intelligent Solutions is the dominant SaaS platform for the multi-trillion-dollar P&C insurance economy, with 99% gross retention, 12% revenue growth, and a forward P/E of just 12.9x — one of the cheapest AI-revenue-generating SaaS companies in the market [1]. AI revenue of ~$100M across 125+ insurers with utilization still in low single digits provides a massive growth runway without requiring new customer acquisition [1]. The EvolutionIQ acquisition extends the platform into disability and workers' comp, 9 of the top 15 U.S. disability carriers are already on board. Structural industry tailwinds (labor shortages, medical inflation, claim complexity) make AI automation a necessity, not a luxury, driving long-term demand independent of economic cycles [1].
thesis_continued: The bear case includes decelerating growth guidance (9% for 2026 vs 12% in 2025), GAAP profitability near zero despite $1B+ revenue, and elevated SBC at 17% of revenue [1]. The stock's 4.2% decline from its 50-day SMA and $6.18 price (well below the $10+ IPO-era levels) reflect market concerns about growth sustainability. Competition from Verisk, Mitchell, and AI-native insurtechs could erode market share. The P&C insurance industry itself faces cyclical pressures that could slow technology adoption during hard market conditions. Additionally, the $500M buyback may not be enough to offset SBC dilution in the near term.
key_catalysts: AI revenue acceleration from low-single-digit utilization rates — $100M current AI revenue could multiply as adoption deepens across 125+ insurers
EvolutionIQ integration driving TAM expansion into disability and workers' compensation with 9 of top 15 carriers
SBC reduction from 17% to 13% in 2026 with single-digit trajectory by 2027, improving GAAP profitability
key_risks: Growth deceleration from 12% to 9% guided for 2026 could signal maturing core business despite AI tailwinds
GAAP profitability remains near zero due to 17% SBC; dilution may offset the $500M buyback program
AI-native insurtech competitors could offer more modern alternatives to CCC's legacy platform architecture
sources: CCC Intelligent Solutions Q4 and FY2025 Earnings Release; 2026 Guidance, February 2026
CCC Corporate Presentations; CPO Josh Valdez Appointment, January 2026
Yahoo Finance — CCC key statistics, accessed March 2026
